title: Reading and Use of English - Part 6
description: You are going to read an article from which six sentences have been removed. Choose from the sentences A–G the one which fits each gap (37–42). There is one extra sentence which you do not need to use.

content.title: A Brew with History: The Story of Coffee Shops
content.text: The first coffeehouse in the Western world opened in Oxford in 1650, and its successors changed the intellectual landscape of Europe for ever. In an age before newspapers, the coffeehouse was the place where merchants, scholars, politicians and poets gathered to exchange news, argue ideas and form alliances. (37) [37] Estos establecimientos fueron en muchos sentidos el primer espacio público democrático de debate abierto. [/37] Lloyd's of London, the great insurance market, began as a coffeehouse frequented by sailors and ship owners; the London Stock Exchange grew from similar origins. || Coffee itself arrived in Europe via the Ottoman Empire, where coffeehouses had flourished in Istanbul since the fifteenth century. The drink was greeted with suspicion by some — Pope Clement VIII reportedly demanded to taste it before deciding whether to ban it as an infidel beverage. (38) [38] Tras probarlo, el papa lo aprobó entusiastamente y lo declaró apto para los cristianos. [/38] His endorsement helped accelerate its spread across the continent. || The nineteenth century saw the coffeehouse evolve into the café, a more intimate, often literary space. In Vienna, writers and philosophers sat for hours over a single cup, filling notebooks and debating aesthetics. (39) [39] El café vienés se convirtió en incubadora de algunas de las ideas más influyentes de la época. [/39] Sigmund Freud, Gustav Klimt and Leon Trotsky all spent significant time in the same handful of Viennese establishments. || In the United States, the diner and the doughnut shop fulfilled a similar social role through much of the twentieth century. The coffeehouse revival came in the 1990s, led largely by Seattle-based Starbucks. (40) [40] Starbucks no solo popularizó el café de especialidad, sino que también redefinió el concepto de «tercer lugar» entre el hogar y el trabajo. [/40] The idea that a commercial space could serve as a community hub — somewhere to linger, work and socialise without necessarily spending much money — proved enormously influential. || The third-wave coffee movement, which emerged in the early 2000s, pushed back against the corporate model. Independent roasters and baristas began treating coffee with the same reverence that sommeliers apply to wine. (41) [41] El origen del grano, el método de preparación y el perfil de sabor se convirtieron en temas de conversación habituales entre los entusiastas. [/41] A flat white or pour-over coffee, prepared with single-origin beans roasted to a specific profile, became a mark of discernment. || Today, the coffee shop remains one of the most visited public spaces in the world. (42) [42] En la era del trabajo remoto, la cafetería ha cobrado nuevo protagonismo como oficina informal para millones de personas. [/42] Whether it is a grand Viennese establishment or a minimalist specialty bar in Tokyo, the coffeehouse continues to be, as it has always been, a place where the public goes to think.

paragraphs.A: After tasting it, the Pope enthusiastically approved it and declared it suitable for Christians.
paragraphs.B: The Viennese café became an incubator for some of the most influential ideas of the age.
paragraphs.C: These establishments functioned as the first genuinely democratic public spaces for open intellectual debate.
paragraphs.D: Bean origin, preparation method and flavour profile became standard topics of discussion among enthusiasts.
paragraphs.E: The arrival of Italian espresso machines in the 1950s transformed the British café into a meeting point for the emerging youth culture.
paragraphs.F: In the era of remote working, the coffee shop has taken on new significance as an informal office for millions.
paragraphs.G: Starbucks not only popularised specialty coffee but also redefined the notion of a 'third place' between home and work.

questions.explanation: C describes the coffeehouse as the first democratic space for debate, connecting with the examples of Lloyd's and the stock exchange.
A narrates the pope's approval after tasting coffee, closing the anecdote about his initial distrust and explaining the spread of the product.
B qualifies the Viennese café as an intellectual incubator, preparing the list of historical figures who frequented it.
G explains the dual impact of Starbucks on specialty coffee and the concept of the third place, developed in the following sentence.
D describes how the origin and preparation method became conversation topics, specifying the focus of the third wave.
F updates the role of the coffee shop in the era of remote working, closing the article with a contemporary perspective.
